subject: Biology
title: Bacteriophages: Virus vs Bacteria
description: A micro-lesson that makes Bacteriophages: Virus vs Bacteria usable.

hook.text: There are viruses that don't infect us—they infect bacteria. They're called bacteriophages.

buildup.text: Phages look like tiny lunar landers. They inject DNA into bacteria and destroy them from inside.

discovery.text: There are an estimated 10 trillion trillion phages on Earth—more than any other biological entity.

twist.text: Phage therapy could treat antibiotic-resistant infections by sending viruses to kill bacteria.

climax.text: As antibiotic resistance grows, phages are being reconsidered as medicine's next weapon.

punchline.text: The enemy of your enemy might save you.
